title: 1981-04-16_Pat_79.txt
key_claims: Dick refines his ontology moving away from strict Trinitarianism toward an Alexandrian/Philonic emanationist model: God $\rightarrow$ Logos (Blueprint/Idea) $\rightarrow$ The Forms $\rightarrow$ Spatiotemporal Universe.
He maps this process perfectly onto John Scottus Eriugena's four stages of nature:
The universe is not an idea in the mind of God; it *derives from the Logos*, which is the first thought of God. Christ (Logos) is the living information blueprint.
Instead of the soul ascending out of the fallen world (Neoplatonism), Dick's model emphasizes that the Godhead descends to the lowest, most fallen level to rescue the soul and return it to unity.
recurring_concepts: **The Word (Logos)**: Understood not as sound, but as the complex formula, schema, or blueprint on which physical reality is based.
**The MMSK (Macro-Micro Symmetrical Key)**: The dialectic process of the Logos structuring and assimilating reality.
reading_excerpt: Dick refines his ontology moving away from strict Trinitarianism toward an Alexandrian/Philonic emanationist model: God $\rightarrow$ Logos (Blueprint/Idea) $\rightarrow$ The Forms $\rightarrow$ Spatiotemporal Universe.
